test_cases.title: Enable digest mode and receive batched email at configured time
preconditions: User is authenticated and has a valid account with an associated email address
User has digest mode currently disabled
At least two individual event notifications have occurred since the last digest delivery
steps: Navigate to notification settings
Enable daily notification digest mode
Set the digest delivery time to a specific time (e.g., 08:00 AM)
Trigger two or more distinct events that would normally generate individual notifications
Wait until the configured digest delivery time passes
test_cases.expected_result: The user receives exactly one email at the configured time containing all events that occurred since the previous digest delivery, and no individual event notification emails are sent.

test_cases.title: No digest email sent when no events occurred since last digest
preconditions: User is authenticated and has digest mode enabled with a configured delivery time
No events have occurred since the last digest delivery
steps: Ensure no events are triggered between the last digest delivery and the next scheduled delivery time
Wait until the configured digest delivery time passes
Check the user's email inbox
test_cases.expected_result: No digest email is sent to the user for that day.

test_cases.title: User can configure the daily digest delivery time
steps: Navigate to notification settings
Change the digest delivery time from the current value to a new specific time (e.g., 06:00 PM)
Save the updated setting
Trigger at least one event
Wait until the newly configured delivery time passes
test_cases.expected_result: The digest email is delivered at the newly configured time, not the previous time.

test_cases.title: Digest includes only events since the previous digest delivery
preconditions: User is authenticated and has digest mode enabled
A prior digest was delivered at time T
Events E1 and E2 occurred before time T (included in the previous digest)
Events E3 and E4 occurred after time T
steps: Allow events E3 and E4 to occur after the last digest delivery
Wait until the next configured digest delivery time passes
Open the received digest email
test_cases.expected_result: The digest email contains only events E3 and E4, and does not include events E1 or E2.

test_cases.title: Individual event notifications are suppressed while digest mode is enabled
preconditions: User is authenticated and has digest mode enabled
Digest delivery time is set to a future time (e.g., several hours away)
steps: Trigger an event that would normally generate an individual notification email
Immediately check the user's email inbox
test_cases.expected_result: No individual event notification email is delivered to the user immediately after the event; the event is held for the next digest.

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated user cannot enable or modify digest mode settings
preconditions: No user is currently authenticated (user is logged out)
steps: Attempt to access the notification settings page or call the API endpoint to enable digest mode without a valid authentication token
Attempt to set a digest delivery time
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request with an authentication error and does not modify any digest mode settings.

assumptions: Digest mode is a per-user toggle setting stored in the user's notification preferences.
The delivery time is configured in the user's local timezone; the system handles timezone-aware scheduling.
Enabling digest mode suppresses all individual event notification emails for that user.
The system defines 'previous digest delivery' as the timestamp of the last successfully sent digest; if no digest has ever been sent, it is assumed to be the time digest mode was first enabled.
Email is the only delivery channel for the digest as stated; other channels (push, SMS) are out of scope for this requirement.
Events are queued reliably and not lost if no digest is sent on a given day.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) how 'events' are defined or what types of events qualify for digest inclusion; (2) the format or structure of the digest email content; (3) behavior when the user changes the delivery time after events have already been queued for the current day; (4) what happens if the digest delivery fails (retry behavior); (5) whether digest mode applies globally or can be scoped to specific event types; (6) the granularity of the delivery time configuration (e.g., hour-only vs. minute-level). These gaps may require additional test cases once clarified.
